steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The function is $z = (0.836)^{2w}$. We need the percent decrease in $z$ for each 1-unit increase in $w$. That means we need the ratio $z(w+1)/z(w)$, then convert to a percent change.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 0.164: this is the one-step decrease for the base $0.836$, but the exponent is $2w$, so the multiplier per step is $(0.836)^2$, not $0.836$. ✗
  • Choice B — 0.836: this is the base of the exponential expression, not the percent decrease. ✗
  • Choice C — 16.4: this would be the percent decrease if the multiplier per step were $0.836$, but the actual multiplier is $(0.836)^2$. ✗
  • Choice D — 30.1: $(0.836)^2 = 0.698896$, so $z$ retains 69.89% each step — a decrease of $1 - 0.6989 = 0.3011 \approx 30.1\%$. ✓
D is correct.
